title: Shri Prem Mandir, Vrindavan
intro: Prem Mandir, located in Vrindavan, is a breathtaking temple dedicated to the divine love of Shri Radha-Krishna and Shri Sita-Ram.
description:
Prem Mandir – Vrindavan, Uttar Pradesh
Introduction
Prem Mandir, located in Vrindavan, is a breathtaking temple dedicated to the divine love of Shri Radha-Krishna and Shri Sita-Ram. Built by Jagadguru Shri Kripalu Ji Maharaj, it is a magnificent architectural marvel made of white Italian marble. The temple is not just a place of worship but a visual masterpiece that attracts thousands of tourists and devotees every day.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Prem Mandir (Temple of Love)
 Location: Vrindavan, Mathura District, Uttar Pradesh 281121
 Presiding Deities: Shri Radha-Krishna and Shri Sita-Ram
 Founder: Jagadguru Shri Kripalu Ji Maharaj
 Significance: Known for architectural beauty and spiritual guidance.
History and Significance
Prem Mandir was established to spread the philosophy of Bhakti (devotion). The construction began in 2001 and was completed in 2012. The temple walls are intricately carved with scenes from the Leelas (pastimes) of Lord Krishna, making the temple a beautiful representation of devotion in stone.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Delhi (IGI Airport): The nearest major international airport (approx. 150 km).
 Agra (Kheria Airport): Approx. 70 km away.
🚆 By Train:
 Mathura Junction: The nearest major railway station. From there, taxis, auto-rickshaws, and buses are easily available to reach Prem Mandir in Vrindavan.
🛣️ By Road:
 Vrindavan is well-connected via the Yamuna Expressway from Delhi and Agra. Public transport and private taxis are readily available.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is cool and pleasant.
 Evening: A visit in the evening is highly recommended to witness the stunning lighting display that changes colors on the marble structure.
Temple Timings
🕒 General Timings: 5:00 AM to 8:30 PM (The evening light show usually starts after sunset).
Main Festivals
 Janmashtami: Celebrated with grandeur.
 Radhashtami: A special day of devotion at the temple.
 Holi: Celebrated with great enthusiasm in the Braj region.
Nearby Attractions
 Banke Bihari Temple: A very sacred temple in Vrindavan.
 ISKCON Temple (Sri Krishna-Balaram Mandir): Famous for its peaceful atmosphere.
 Nidhivan: A mysterious and sacred forest.
 Mathura Janmabhoomi: The birthplace of Lord Krishna.
Dress Code
 As it is a spiritual site, modest and traditional attire is recommended.
Facilities for Devotees
 Beautiful gardens and water fountains.
 Parking facilities.
 Canteen and food court.
 Clean premises and organized movement for visitors.
Conclusion
Prem Mandir is a true synthesis of spiritual devotion and architectural brilliance. The serene atmosphere, combined with the evening illuminations, offers a deeply calming and joyful experience for every visitor.

faq.q1: To which deity is Prem Mandir dedicated?
faq.a1: Prem Mandir is dedicated to Shri Radha-Krishna and Shri Sita-Ram.
faq.a2: It is located in Vrindavan, Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh.
faq.a3: Prem Mandir was built by Jagadguru Shri Kripalu Ji Maharaj.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit Prem Mandir?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel.
faq.q5: What is Prem Mandir famous for?
faq.a5: It is famous for its beautiful white marble architecture, stunning light displays (illuminations), and peaceful spiritual atmosphere.
